title: Camera Feed Loss and Recording Gap Detection
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="vms:stream_health"
| where fps=0 OR recording_gap_sec > 60 OR stream_state="lost"
| stats latest(recording_gap_sec) as gap by camera_id, site
| sort -gap
description: Detects loss of RTSP/HLS stream and gaps in NVR recording separately from generic camera ‚Äúoffline‚Äù pings.
value: Detects loss of RTSP/HLS stream and gaps in NVR recording separately from generic camera ‚Äúoffline‚Äù pings.
implementation: Poll VMS for per-camera FPS and last recorded frame time. Alert on stream loss or gap >1 min for critical zones.
visualization: Table (cameras with feed loss), Timeline (gaps), Floor plan overlay.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: VMS API, stream health probes.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="vms:stream_health"` (fps, keyframes, recording_continuity).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Poll VMS for per-camera FPS and last recorded frame time. Alert on stream loss or gap >1 min for critical zones.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="vms:stream_health"
| where fps=0 OR recording_gap_sec > 60 OR stream_state="lost"
| stats latest(recording_gap_sec) as gap by camera_id, site
| sort -gap
```

Understanding this SPL

**Camera Feed Loss and Recording Gap Detection** ‚Äî Detects loss of RTSP/HLS stream and gaps in NVR recording separately from generic camera ‚Äúoffline‚Äù pings.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="vms:stream_health"` (fps, keyframes, recording_continuity). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): VMS API, stream health probes. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: vms:stream_health. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="vms:stream_health". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where fps=0 OR recording_gap_sec > 60 OR stream_state="lost"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by camera_id, site** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (cameras with feed loss), Timeline (gaps), Floor plan overlay.
grandmaExplanation: We catch loss of RTSP/HLS stream and gaps in NVR recording separately from generic camera ‚Äúoffline‚Äù pings ‚Äî so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
